HelpComment: Commands entered into Line 1 are of the form '[Asset String] (space) [Command' and are run on pressing Enter
is a semicolon delimited set of Equities, ETFS, currencies, indices, or user data.
is a space delimited series of Analytics to run and possible parameters
1) A list of commands is shown below:
2) Commands which start with `AV.[command]` have no asset strings.
3) Commands that end with "2" are time series graphs that are shown below the main graphs
4) Some commands show comparisons with the asset in the second line
5) Some results may be in a separate tab, whose name is typically changed to highlight its existance
plots a rebased time series (i.e. starting at 100) of the equity IBM and the ETF QQQ below whatever is in the first graph.
plots an (e.g. 2yr) time series of USD/BRL rebased to 6 months prior to today
shows this help screen, and all functions relevant to Equities
Shows a table of all historical data saved internally.
plots returns of both IBM and QQQ against the second line (e.g SPY)
Text of the form <startperiod>::<endperiod> where either can be blank to indicate today().
Either is of the form [+/-][nnn][d|m|y]  OR [yyyy-mm-dd] where the first is a relative number of periods from today(), and the second is an actual date.
A one month period st arting 3 months ago.
A period from 2 years ago to today.
A period from 2 years ago to one year ahead of today()
A comma delimited string of four items to filter options
[F|B|A] where `F` is first maturity, `B` is second maturity, `A` (or blank) are all maturities
[M|Q|A] where `M` is for monthly contracts, `Q` for quarterly,  `A` (or blank) are all
[C|P|A] for [C]alls,[P]uts, or [A] all.  Blank is all
[otm|itm|A] Out of the money, in the money or both
[act|A] If `A`, only pass contracts with positive open interest
filters the second monthly maturity (from today) Out of the Money Puts
